title: Community Shelters * Homeless Veterans - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Community Shelters * Homeless Veterans 8 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Ex-Offenders Homeless Couples Homeless Families Homeless Men Homeless Veterans Homeless Women Homeless Youth Mothers Pregnant Individuals Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BOISE RESCUE MISSION MINISTRIES CITY LIGHT HOME FOR WOMEN AND CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers meals and emergency shelter. Shelter services include clothing provision, education, work-search assistance, mental health counseling, addiction recovery through the New Life Program, children’s programs, and coordination services with Boise Veteran's Affairs for homeless female veterans. Eligible community members will not be turned away for food or a clean bed due to lack of space. 1404 W Jefferson Street Boise, ID 83702 Eligibility: Women experiencing homelessness, women with children, and women who are veterans Hours: Shelter check in: 7 days per week 4pm Breakfast: Sunday-Friday 6am, Saturday 8am Lunch: 7 days per week noon Dinner: 7 days per week 5:30pm Email (208) 368-9901 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOISE RESCUE MISSION MINISTRIES RIVER OF LIFE MEN'S SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers emergency meals, shelter, and clothing. Shelter services include education, job search assistance, and mental health counseling. 575 S 13th Street Boise, ID 83702 Eligibility: Shelter and clothing: Men experiencing homelessness with a focus on veterans Meals: Unrestricted Hours: Shelter: Check in 4pm-5:30pm Dinner: 5pm for community guests including women and children Email (208) 389-9840 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY ACTION COLUMBIA INN EMERGENCY SHELTER COLUMBIA INN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community shelter with up to 64 beds. 495 Marine Drive Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Families with children experiencing homelessness, individuals and families escaping domestic violence, and youth age 18-24 Prioritizes families with children, individuals with mobility-affecting disabilities, and individuals who are part of the Latinx, 2SLGBTQIA+, and Veteran communities Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8:30am-3:30pm Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 325-1400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COUNTY HUMAN SERVICE DIVISION LANE COUNTY SHELTER FINDER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter locator with bed availability in Lane County. Not all shelters in Lane County are listed. 151 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by shelter Hours: Not applicable (541) 682-3798 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS LAURELWOOD CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Shelter and service center for up to 120 people experiencing homelessness. The site has a space for coffee and tea, books and board games, a gated courtyard, showers and laundry, space to leave belongings during the day and provides onsite meals. Staff will provide housing-focused support, employment and benefits assistance, and health-focused services. Beds at a shelter are not guaranteed. 6130 SE Foster Road Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older or couples (any two people who present as a couple, regardless of gender) who are experiencing homelessness. Priority is given to people age 55 and older, adults with disabilities, and veterans. Pets allowed; must be housebroken, and the owner is responsible for caring for, feeding, and cleaning up after the animal Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS RIVER DISTRICT NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Navigation Center is a temporary, low-barrier shelter serving up to 90 adults. The site has a space for coffee and tea, books and board games, a gated courtyard, showers and laundry, space to leave belongings during the day, and provides onsite meals. Staff will provide housing-focused support, employment and benefits assistance, and health-focused services. 1111 NW Naito Parkway Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals age 18 and older experiencing homelessness, with priority for individuals age 55 and older, adults with disabilities, and veterans. Pets allowed; must be housebroken, and the owner is responsible for caring for, feeding, and cleaning up after the animal Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close Resource Center walk-in hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (650 NW Irving Street, Portland, OR 97209) (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS WALNUT PARK SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a low-barrier shelter. Residents will have access to coffee, tea, books, and board games. Bathrooms, showers, and laundry onsite are available for program participants. Beds at a shelter are not guaranteed. 5329 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Age 18 and older, with priority for individuals age 55 and older, adults with disabilities, and veterans Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS WILLAMETTE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Shelter and service center for up to 120 people experiencing homelessness. The site has 2 large sleeping rooms, a space for coffee and tea, books and board games, a gated courtyard, showers and laundry, space to leave belongings during the day, and a kitchen for light food and beverages. Staff will provide information, referral, and support. Onsite support services include housing, health, and income resources. Beds at a shelter are not guaranteed. 5120 SE Milwaukie Avenue Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older or couples (any two people who present as a couple, regardless of gender). Priority is given to people age 55 and older, adults with disabilities, and veterans. Pets allowed; must be housebroken, and the owner is responsible for caring for, feeding, and cleaning up after the animal Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY ACTION COLUMBIA INN LANE COUNTY HUMAN SERVICE DIVISION LANE COUNTY SHELTER FINDER TRANSITION PROJECTS LAURELWOOD CENTER TRANSITION PROJECTS RIVER DISTRICT NAVIGATION CENTER TRANSITION PROJECTS WALNUT PARK SHELTER TRANSITION PROJECTS WILLAMETTE CENTER
